sections.Description:
Alpha Burrowers move underground to arrive at their destination and bypass any obstacles, much like aLeaperminus the jumping. When emerging they attack a 3-tile wide area in front of them perpendicular to where they are facing. Upon taking any damage, they will cancel their attack and retreat underground, re-emerging elsewhere (within 4 tiles of where they retreated from) on their next turn. Alpha Burrowers can not end their move on top ofIce Tiles.
Alpha Burrowers areStableand thus cannot be pushed or moved with physical force, however they can still be relocated by theControl Mech'sControl Shot weapon.
As rare enemies, Alpha Burrowers cannot appear on the first island and are limited to two spawns per mission.
sections.Strategy: Use the same strategy as for fightingBurrowers, though Alpha Burrowers having 5HP does make them a lot sturdier. Try to bump other enemies into them if you cannot take them down with one strong hit, as they will still retreat from any kind of damage, and block enemy spawns to prevent being overwhelmed the next turn by the Alpha Burrower emerging back.

full_text:
Alpha Burrower
Details
Health
5
Move
4
Systems
Bladed Carapace
(
Enemy
)
Effect
Slam against 3 tiles in a row, hitting each for 2 damage.
Special
Burrower
: This Vek will hide underground after taking one or more damage.
Stable
: Cannot be moved by any weapon effect (Push, Teleport, etc.).
Alpha Burrower
is one of the Alpha
Vek
from
Into The Breach
.
Description
[
]
Alpha Burrowers move underground to arrive at their destination and bypass any obstacles, much like a
Leaper
minus the jumping. When emerging they attack a 3-tile wide area in front of them perpendicular to where they are facing. Upon taking any damage, they will cancel their attack and retreat underground, re-emerging elsewhere (within 4 tiles of where they retreated from) on their next turn. Alpha Burrowers can not end their move on top of
Ice Tiles
.
Alpha Burrowers are
Stable
and thus cannot be pushed or moved with physical force, however they can still be relocated by the
Control Mech's
Control Shot weapon.
As rare enemies, Alpha Burrowers cannot appear on the first island and are limited to two spawns per mission.
Strategy
[
]
Use the same strategy as for fighting
Burrowers
, though Alpha Burrowers having 5HP does make them a lot sturdier. Try to bump other enemies into them if you cannot take them down with one strong hit, as they will still retreat from any kind of damage, and block enemy spawns to prevent being overwhelmed the next turn by the Alpha Burrower emerging back.
